title: NVD CVE API 2.0 ingestion source
text: The National Vulnerability Database CVE API is the authoritative JSON REST source used by this platform for CVE records. The API supports retrieving individual CVEs or CVE collections, offset-based pagination, CVE ID filters, CPE filters, keyword search, CVSS severity filters, CWE filters, and Known Exploited Vulnerability filters. SOC workflows should preserve CVE ID, publication and modification dates, CVSS metrics, CWE mappings, affected products, references, and KEV context when available.

metadata.retrieval_hint: Use https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0 with scoped filters and pagination.

title: MITRE ATT&CK STIX 2.1 automation source
text: MITRE ATT&CK data should be ingested from the official STIX 2.1 dataset or the official ATT&CK TAXII server for automated workflows. STIX is the most granular machine-readable representation of ATT&CK and includes techniques, tactics, mitigations, groups, software, campaigns, relationships, and citations. SOC workflows should retain ATT&CK IDs, object type, name, description, platforms, kill-chain phases, revoked/deprecated status, relationships, and source references.

metadata.retrieval_hint: Prefer official STIX 2.1 or TAXII for automated updates.

title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 source
text: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 is the current NIST resource for helping organizations understand and improve cybersecurity risk management. The platform should index CSF 2.0 functions, categories, subcategories, profiles, informative references, and quick-start guidance so executive and compliance reports can cite governance, identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover outcomes.

metadata.retrieval_hint: Use the CSF 2.0 resource center and official PDF/tool exports for production ingestion.

title: Credential access failed-login triage playbook
text: When repeated failed authentication events are followed by a successful login, analysts should validate the actor, source IP, device posture, impossible travel signals, MFA status, and recent privilege changes. Recommended response actions include increasing incident priority when the account is privileged, opening an investigation, preserving authentication logs, resetting credentials when compromise is likely, and documenting evidence with timestamps and affected assets.
